summary: Unfading is a native iOS SwiftUI map diary for couples and recurring groups, turning shared dates and trips into place-based memory collections with a map-first surface and a synchronized bottom sheet gallery. Resuming from the 2026-04-08 baseline, the current delivery lane has landed M1–M4 across UI and logic worktrees: marker selection state, cluster zoom with reduce-motion fallback, filter-change pin animations, place confirmation gating with InferenceSource, and marker↔sheet sync hardening via selectionRevision. Product focus now is to keep the couple/general-group dual mode intact, protect the event hierarchy Group→DateEvent→Memory→MemoryPost, and ensure inferred place always requires explicit user confirmation before save. Category risk is moderate: the space has public social map apps and private photo journals, but lacks a private spatial memory product that treats the bottom sheet as a first-class browsing surface and adapts curation to both couple and group modes. Next intake→delivery→evaluation loop should validate HIG compliance on map, sheet, and rewind surfaces, and confirm acceptance criteria for Epics 1–4 against native Xcode evidence rather than Expo-only smoke.
user_pains: Chat albums and photo feeds cannot answer 'what happened here with us' without scrolling years of history
Existing map apps optimize for public discovery, not private shared memory tied to place and event
Couples and recurring groups are forced into separate tools or couple-only apps that do not fit group meetups
Revisiting the same place loses layered history because tools do not merge or stack repeat visits
Inferred place from photo metadata can be wrong, and silent auto-save erodes trust in a memory diary
Marker taps and bottom-sheet content drifting out of sync breaks spatial browsing confidence
Rewind and anniversary moments are buried in camera rolls with no place, people, or emotion context
hig_risks: Bottom sheet as primary browsing surface must preserve native sheet gestures, detents, and safe-area insets rather than a custom modal
Map floating controls (filter, current location, add pin) risk oversized or gesture-hostile overlays that block pinch and pan
Cluster zoom and filter animations must honor Reduce Motion; current M3/M4 implementations claim fallbacks but need vision QA confirmation
Place confirmation gating must not use deceptive auto-confirm affordances; the confirm control needs a 44pt target and clear labeling
Dark mode and Dynamic Type coverage on date headers, emotion tags, and memory cards is unverified against HIG
VoiceOver labels for map markers, clusters, and sheet rows are required before release and not yet audited
Rewind notifications and location-based reminders must expose clear user ON/OFF control to avoid App Store review risk around background location
AI-generated UI cannot ship without an HIG audit trail in context_harness/reports, per the harness constitution
design_references: Apple Maps and iPhone-native Maps for map-first spatial confidence and calm hierarchy
Naver Map and Google Maps result panels for bottom-sheet detents and first-class browsing behavior
iPhone Photos app for date and event grouping, memory cards, and restrained chrome
Apple Human Interface Guidelines: Sheets, Maps, Notifications, Accessibility, and Dark Mode sections
context_harness/policies/ios_hig_guardrails.md as the release gate for 44pt targets, safe areas, and VoiceOver
context_harness/prd/ui_ux_screen_contract.md for the non-negotiable map-first and dual-mode product shape
context_harness/architecture/native_ios_strategy.md for preferring Xcode runtime evidence over Expo smoke
